overview: Hammer Time is an explosive aggro/combo deck in Modern that aims for fast combo kills in the early game while retaining a resilient beatdown plan for later stages. The mono-white version avoids fetch lands and shocklands to reduce mana screw and self-damage. It operates as a metagame 'dolphin' strategy, cycling in and out of popularity based on opponent hate levels.
win_conditions: Fast combo kills using Colossus Hammer, Sigarda's Aid, and Stoneforge Mystic
Late-game beatdown plan centered on Urza's Saga
Streak of spells from The Reality Chip

keep_criteria: Lands, Target cards, and Search cards
Keep hands with 1-2 lands plus at least one mana accelerator (Mox Opal, Chrome Mox, Arcane Signet, or Sol Ring) and one cantrip/draw spell.
Keep hands with Colossus Hammer paired with two sources of acceleration or draw.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking a Target despite having Search and Equipment
Hands dependent on topdecking early game
Average hands without a clear plan
Mulligan any hand with three or more lands, no acceleration/draw synergy, or only Hammer with no way to cast it.

examples.reason: God Hand example that can finish the game on turn 2.
Turn one Lotus Bloom with supporting lands — Starting with Lotus Bloom significantly increases the chance of winning on turn four.
Pinnacle Emissary on turn one — Unlocks the hand by enabling early artifact plays and combo pieces.
Generic Affinity hand — Affinity does not mulligan well; it requires a full hand of cheap artifacts to litter the board effectively.

early_game: Aim for explosive combo kills with equipment and targets
Aggressive Mulligan to ensure a short-term plan
Race aggro decks or play around control removals
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Mox Opal or Chrome Mox, then cast a cantrip (Opt, Brainstorm, Ponder, or Gitaxian Rampant) to dig for acceleration or Hammer.
Turn 2: Cast a second mana rock, ensure Opal is active (land + Opal on battlefield), and cast Colossus Hammer if you have the mana.
Sequence mana rocks to enable Opal’s one-mana discount as early as possible.
mid_game: Use protections like Giver of Runes or Sanctifier en-Vec
Play around opponent's removals and hate cards
Utilize Mana Tithe to slow down midrange/control
Prioritize keeping Mox Opal active to reduce spell costs.
Use cantrips to filter into acceleration or Hammer, but avoid over-drawing if you’re low on lands.
Manage your graveyard carefully if facing Rest in Peace or Blood Moon, as your draw engine relies on it.
Commit threats only when you have protection or the opponent is tapped out.
Hold back Hammer if you suspect hard interaction (Red Elemental Blast, Counterspell) and you lack a backup plan.
Use removal/counter to clear the board against aggro before going for the kill.
late_game: Shift to a beatdown plan with Urza's Saga
Colossus Hammer is the primary finisher, often cast on turn one or two.
Secondary finishers include a combination of cheap artifacts, cantrips, and Lightning Axe/Swords to clear the board and go lethal.
Go all-in when you have enough mana and draw spells to guarantee casting Hammer, or when the opponent has no interaction left.
Commit to threats if you can protect them or if going below lethal forces a mistake.

key_cards.name: Colossus Hammer
key_cards.notes: Core combo piece for early game kills

key_cards.name: Sigarda's Aid
key_cards.notes: Enables finding equipment or targets for the combo

key_cards.name: Stoneforge Mystic
key_cards.notes: Primary engine to fetch equipment from the library

key_cards.name: Urza's Saga
key_cards.notes: Enables late-game plan by creating Construct tokens

key_cards.name: The Reality Chip
key_cards.notes: Can win games with a streak of spells from the top

key_cards.name: Sanctifier en-Vec
key_cards.notes: Defensive threat that disrupts graveyard strategies and survives removal

key_cards.name: Shadowspear
key_cards.notes: Key for racing aggro decks and applying pressure against Burn

key_cards.name: Mana Tithe
key_cards.notes: Slows down opponents and buys time to assemble combo or beatdown

sequencing_tips: Hold off on the combo initially to handle early threats with March of Otherworldly Light
Play around opponent removals before committing to the combo
Use protections or wait for opponent to tap out before going all-in
Distribute damage rather than attacking for lethal if opponent has open mana
Save Mana Tithe and Pithing Needle for key combo pieces or hate cards
Hold Engineered Explosives until you are ready to win the game, as it destroys your own zero-cost artifacts. Only use it as a desperation play to stop a resolving Lotus Bloom.
Lead with a turn one Lotus Bloom to maximize the probability of resolving a turn four win condition.
Use Consign to Memory to counter the triggered ability of Oza, the Thousand Faces, delaying the search for Pithing Needle and buying time for Lotus Blooms to resolve.
Mox Opal requires a land on the battlefield to activate
Watch for Blood Moon, Rest in Peace, and Red Elemental Blast, as they can completely shut down the deck.
Don’t overcommit to threats if you can’t protect them, and always keep a backup plan if Hammer is countered.
The deck’s high variance means practice and metagame prediction are key to consistent results.

matchups.advice: Hold off initial threats, play Sanctifier en-Vec for defense/offense, use Pithing Needle on Engineered Explosives.
Focus on Urza's Saga plan for constant threats, play around free removals, don't miss attacks.
On Play, race with combo before turn 4. On Draw, play reactively with Drannith Magistrate and Mana Tithe.
Hold initial pressure, race with Shadowspear, distribute damage to avoid Deflecting Palm.
Attack quickly, play around artifact hate, use Path/March on small combo pieces, save Tithe/Needle for Yawgmoth.

Lead with Oza, the Thousand Faces on turn one to find Pithing Needle by turn three and name Goblin Charbelcher. (from gameplay) If Pithing Needle resolves, use Sink into Stupor to bounce it, then untap and win with Goblin Charbelcher. (from gameplay) Monitor the opponent's mana base; if they lack blue mana, they cannot cast counter spells, allowing you to play aggressively without fear of interaction. (from gameplay)

Use Spell Pierce or cheap cantrips to dig around counters. Cast Hammer when the opponent is tapped out or after they’ve used interaction on threats. Sometimes use cheap artifacts as bait to force counters before casting Hammer.
Race them aggressively. Use removal and counters to disrupt their mana sources. Don’t let them stabilize; pressure early and finish before their late-game threats come online.

Stabilize with Lightning Axe, Spell Pierce, Counterspell, or Swords to Plowshares. Don’t overcommit to Hammer if you can’t clear the board. Use cantrips to find answers and stabilize before going for the kill.

Apply early pressure to force interaction. Use cantrips to dig for answers or Hammer. Don’t let them stabilize; if they do, use Lightning Axe or Swords to clear threats and go for lethal. Sometimes you must go below lethal with threats to force a mistake.

Win the interaction war. Use Spell Pierce and Counterspell to protect your Hammer and disrupt theirs. Prioritize removal on their threats and dig deeper for Hammer. Track their hand for counters and play around them with cheap artifacts or timing.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells, discard, and board wipes.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Hammer Time brings one of the fastest combo-kills in Modern, but with the resilience to have a great beatdown plan on later stages of the game.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: you can combo with the right cards in the Early Game or go to a beatdown plan with Urza's Saga in the late-game.

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: The idea here is to hold off a possible initial threat with March of Otherworldly Light before going into the combo.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: opponent can only deal with it playing Engineered Explosives
Deflecting Palm : that's the card you have to always be playing around
Force of Vigor , Outland Liberator and often Boseiju, Who Endures
